title: hoya odorata subsp. loheri
intro: Hoya odorata subsp. loheri, also known as the Philippine waxflower or Loher's hoya, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its fragrant, star-shaped flowers and waxy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Hoya odorata subsp. loheri include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional remedies for various ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
